ground.schema:
Node properties:
- **Movie**
  - `average_vote`: FLOAT Example: "7.7"
  - `overview`: STRING Example: "Led by Woody, Andy's toys live happily in his room"
  - `revenue`: FLOAT Example: "3.73554033E8"
  - `vote_count`: INTEGER Example: "5415"
  - `tagline`: STRING Example: "Roll the dice and unleash the excitement!"
  - `budget`: INTEGER Example: "30000000"
  - `title`: STRING Example: "Toy Story"
  - `poster_path`: STRING Example: "/rhIRbceoE9lR4veEXuwCC2wARtG.jpg"
  - `imdb_id`: STRING Example: "tt0114709"
  - `status`: STRING Example: "Released"
  - `runtime`: FLOAT Example: "81.0"
  - `popularity`: FLOAT Example: "21.946943"
  - `homepage`: STRING Example: "http://toystory.disney.com/toy-story"
  - `release_date`: DATE Example: "1995-10-30"
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 862, Max: 31357
  - `original_title`: STRING Example: "Toy Story"
  - `original_language`: STRING Example: "en"
- **Language**
  - `id`: STRING Example: "en"
  - `name`: STRING Available options: ['', 'Gaeilge', 'Kiswahili', 'Azərbaycan', 'Somali', 'беларуская мова', 'Fulfulde', 'Hausa']
- **Country**
  - `id`: STRING Example: "US"
  - `name`: STRING Example: "United States of America"
- **Genre**
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 12, Max: 10770
  - `name`: STRING Example: "Animation"
- **ProductionCompany**
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 3, Max: 10201
  - `name`: STRING Example: "Pixar Animation Studios"
- **Collection**
  - `name`: STRING Example: "Toy Story Collection"
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 10, Max: 480160
  - `backdrop_path`: STRING Example: "/9FBwqcd9IRruEDUrTdcaafOMKUq.jpg"
  - `poster_path`: STRING Example: "/7G9915LfUQ2lVfwMEEhDsn3kT4B.jpg"
- **Person**
  - `gender`: INTEGER Example: "2"
  - `profile_path`: STRING Example: "/pQFoyx7rp09CJTAb932F2g8Nlho.jpg"
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 31, Max: 12900
  - `name`: STRING Example: "Tom Hanks"
- **User**
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 27490cf6-22eb-495d-9dd6-902c0f89c627, Max: 4
- **Keyword**
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 931, Max: 9713
  - `name`: STRING Example: "jealousy"
- **Video**
  - `tagline`: STRING Example: "Fresh Fish. Mixed Vegetables."
  - `average_vote`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 10.0
  - `overview`: STRING Example: "Get ready as Bob the Tomato, Larry the Cucumber an"
  - `revenue`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 0.0
  - `poster_path`: STRING Example: "/iWahPsY3hsKOizZHgzQcAxv4O3B.jpg"
  - `vote_count`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 115
  - `title`: STRING Example: "Jonah: A VeggieTales Movie"
  - `release_date`: DATE Min: 1973-01-12, Max: 2017-03-17
  - `status`: STRING Available options: ['Released']
  - `budget`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 2219684
  - `popularity`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 9.22546
  - `imdb_id`: STRING Example: "tt0298388"
  - `original_title`: STRING Example: "Jonah: A VeggieTales Movie"
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 13284, Max: 461088
  - `runtime`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 583.0
  - `original_language`: STRING Example: "en"
  - `homepage`: STRING Example: "http://www.loosechange911.com/"
- **Adult**
  - `average_vote`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 6.3
  - `overview`: STRING Available options: ['A sailor takes an American businessman and his gir', 'The FBI and Texas police make ready to storm the h', 'Electrical Girl centers around a horny young woman', 'Ágata suffers from a psychological disorder which ', "Shane Ryan's sequel to the disturbing Amateur Porn", 'Australian film about a fictional sub-par Australi', 'Olaf and his mother run a boarding house and a whi', 'A man who returns home to find his wife cheating o', 'An American scientific research facility opens a p']
  - `revenue`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 0.0
  - `poster_path`: STRING Available options: ['/tzVK7nTnzUiVeFSZhOAIHmrgXUS.jpg', '/1VzwIa8HTykNiCCW8PBaJnyTvO8.jpg', '/yTfqRQlJMTAuTNDG01XjNg5KPPg.jpg', '/oP2WvKIWxqSBVHjfYAizFHOqBLG.jpg', '/zpycBSiBFp5Uib6gWJpgEvfOgdn.jpg', '/e7CAc8OadcUoKNPIX86SEPSX7f.jpg', '/jjncR0vECvXVrBvr0A1vLghL4pN.jpg', '/pJLmZBruOmn2c9mYWe3h1xcXaA0.jpg', '/3uv9oaqOJkkdZiQp6r9lf24tkHW.jpg']
  - `vote_count`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 16
  - `title`: STRING Available options: ['Erotic Nights of the Living Dead', 'Standoff', 'Electrical Girl', 'Diet of Sex', 'Amateur Porn Star Killer 2', 'The Band', 'The Sinful Dwarf', 'Adulterers', 'Half -Life']
  - `release_date`: DATE Min: 1973-12-20, Max: 2016-01-05
  - `status`: STRING Available options: ['Released', 'In Production']
  - `budget`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 750000
  - `popularity`: FLOAT Min: 0.001803, Max: 6.889832
  - `imdb_id`: STRING Available options: ['tt0079642', 'tt0119931', 'tt0322232', 'tt3094816', 'tt1161951', 'tt1153101', 'tt0070696', 'tt4044464', 'tt2697688']
  - `original_title`: STRING Available options: ['Le notti erotiche dei morti viventi', 'Standoff', '發電悄嬌娃', 'Diet of Sex', 'Amateur Porn Star Killer 2', 'The Band', 'Dværgen', 'Adulterers', 'Half -Life']
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 5422, Max: 448290
  - `runtime`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 112.0
  - `original_language`: STRING Available options: ['it', 'en', 'cn', 'es', 'da']
  - `tagline`: STRING Available options: ['Trust Will Get You Killed', 'Comedy, food, drama and sex, a lot of sex', 'Humiliation. Rape. Murder. You know the drill.', "Sex, drugs and Rock 'n Roll", 'The Mother of all Dwarfsploitation films!', 'Love. Betrayal. Vengeance']
  - `homepage`: STRING Available options: ['http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0119931/', 'http://www.dietofsex.com/']
- **Package**
  - `price`: FLOAT Min: 0.0, Max: 99.99
  - `duration`: DURATION 
  - `id`: INTEGER Min: 0, Max: 6
  - `name`: STRING Available options: ['Childrens', 'Bronze', 'Silver', 'Gold', 'Platinum', 'Ultimate', 'Free Trial']
- **Subscription**
  - `expiresAt`: DATE_TIME Min: 2020-08-23T16:20:52.383Z, Max: 2020-08-23T16:20:52.383Z
  - `id`: STRING Available options: ['e74f7163-eace-4169-8043-41627f1fe236']
Relationship properties:
- **CAST_FOR**
  - `credit_id: STRING` Example: "52fe4459c3a36847f8090d7d"
  - `order: INTEGER` Example: "0"
  - `character: STRING` Example: "Charlie Wilson"
  - `cast_id: INTEGER` Example: "12"
- **CREW_FOR**
  - `credit_id: STRING` Example: "55e4f0abc3a368140800047e"
  - `job: STRING` Example: "Producer"
  - `department: STRING` Example: "Production"
- **RATED**
  - `timestamp: DATE_TIME` Example: "2015-03-09T22:49:42Z"
  - `rating: FLOAT` Example: "5.0"
The relationships:
(:Movie)-[:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Movie)-[:SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Movie)-[:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(:Country)
(:Movie)-[:IN_GENRE]->(:Genre)
(:Movie)-[:PRODUCED_BY]->(:ProductionCompany)
(:Movie)-[:HAS_KEYWORD]->(:Keyword)
(:Movie)-[:IN_COLLECTION]->(:Collection)
(:Person)-[:CAST_FOR]->(:Movie)
(:Person)-[:CREW_FOR]->(:Movie)
(:Person)-[:CREW_FOR]->(:Video)
(:User)-[:RATED]->(:Movie)
(:User)-[:RATED]->(:Video)
(:Video)-[:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Video)-[:SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Video)-[:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(:Country)
(:Video)-[:IN_GENRE]->(:Genre)
(:Video)-[:PRODUCED_BY]->(:ProductionCompany)
(:Video)-[:HAS_KEYWORD]->(:Keyword)
(:Video)-[:IN_COLLECTION]->(:Collection)
(:Adult)-[:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Adult)-[:SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(:Language)
(:Adult)-[:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(:Country)
(:Adult)-[:IN_GENRE]->(:Genre)
(:Adult)-[:PRODUCED_BY]->(:ProductionCompany)
(:Adult)-[:HAS_KEYWORD]->(:Keyword)
(:Package)-[:PROVIDES_ACCESS_TO]->(:Genre)
(:Subscription)-[:FOR_PACKAGE]->(:Package)
ground.cypher:
MATCH
  p1 = (m:Movie)-[r1:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(l:Language),
  p2 = m-[r2:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(c:Country),
  p3 = m-[r3:IN_GENRE]->(g:Genre),
  p4 = m-[r4:PRODUCED_BY]->(pc:ProductionCompany),
  p5 = m-[r5:HAS_KEYWORD]->(k:Keyword),
  p6 = m-[r6:IN_COLLECTION]->(col:Collection),
  p7 = (p:Person)-[r7:CAST_FOR]->m,
  p8 = (u:User)-[r8:RATED]->m
OPTIONAL MATCH
  p9 = (m)-[r9:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(l2:Language),
  p10 = m-[r10:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(c2:Country),
  p11 = m-[r11:IN_GENRE]->(g2:Genre),
  p12 = m-[r12:PRODUCED_BY]->(pc2:ProductionCompany),
  p13 = m-[r13:HAS_KEYWORD]->(k2:Keyword),
  p14 = m-[r14:IN_COLLECTION]->(col2:Collection),
  p15 = (p:Person)-[r15:CREW_FOR]->m,
  p16 = (p:Person)-[r16:CREW_FOR]->(v:Video),
  p17 = (u:User)-[r17:RATED]->(v:Video)
RETURN
  m.title AS movie_title,
  collect(DISTINCT l.name AS language) AS languages,
  c.name AS production_country,
  collect(DISTINCT g.name AS genres) AS genres,
  pc.name AS production_company,
  collect(DISTINCT k.name AS keywords) AS keywords,
  col.name AS collection_name,
  collect(DISTINCT p.name AS cast_actors) AS cast_actors,
  collect(DISTINCT p.name AS crew_members) AS crew_members,
  collect(DISTINCT u.username AS users_rating_movie) AS users_rating_movie,
  collect(DISTINCT v.title AS video_titles) AS video_titles,
  collect(DISTINCT u.username AS users_rating_video) AS users_rating_video;
ground.question:
Here’s a refined and optimized Cypher query based on your requirements, ensuring clarity and correctness while handling optional relationships and aggregations:

```cypher
MATCH
  // Core Movie Data
  (m:Movie)-[r1:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(l:Language),
  (m)-[r2:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(c:Country),
  (m)-[r3:IN_GENRE]->(g:Genre),
  (m)-[r4:PRODUCED_BY]->(pc:ProductionCompany),
  (m)-[r5:HAS_KEYWORD]->(k:Keyword),
  (m)-[r6:IN_COLLECTION]->(col:Collection),

  // Cast & Crew
  (p:Person)-[r7:CAST_FOR]->m AS cast_actors,
  (p)-[r8:CREW_FOR]->m AS crew_members,

  // User Ratings (Movie)
  (u:User)-[r9:RATED]->m AS users_rating_movie,

  // Optional Video Data (if exists)
  OPTIONAL MATCH
    (m)-[r10:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(l2:Language),
    (m)-[r11:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(c2:Country),
    (m)-[r12:IN_GENRE]->(g2:Genre),
    (m)-[r13:PRODUCED_BY]->(pc2:ProductionCompany),
    (m)-[r14:HAS_KEYWORD]->(k2:Keyword),
    (m)-[r15:IN_COLLECTION]->(col2:Collection),
    (p:Person)-[r16:CREW_FOR]->(v:Video) AS video_crew,
    (u:User)-[r17:RATED]->(v:Video) AS users_rating_video,

    // Video Titles
    (v)-[:TITLE]->(v_title:Video) WITH v_title.title AS video_titles,

    // Optional: Collect distinct relationships
    // (e.g., for filtering in the RETURN clause)
    // (m)-[r18:...]->(...)

RETURN
  m.title AS movie_title,

  // Languages (union of both sets)
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN l.name IS NOT NULL THEN l.name ELSE null END) AS languages,
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN l2.name IS NOT NULL THEN l2.name ELSE null END) AS languages_optional,

  // Production Country (union)
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c.name IS NOT NULL THEN c.name ELSE null END) AS production_country,
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c2.name IS NOT NULL THEN c2.name ELSE null END) AS production_country_optional,

  // Genres (union)
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN g.name IS NOT NULL THEN g.name ELSE null END) AS genres,
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN g2.name IS NOT NULL THEN g2.name ELSE null END) AS genres_optional,

  // Production Company (union)
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN pc.name IS NOT NULL THEN pc.name ELSE null END) AS production_company,
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN pc2.name IS NOT NULL THEN pc2.name ELSE null END) AS production_company_optional,

  // Keywords (union)
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN k.name IS NOT NULL THEN k.name ELSE null END) AS keywords,
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN k2.name IS NOT NULL THEN k2.name ELSE null END) AS keywords_optional,

  // Collections (union)
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN col.name IS NOT NULL THEN col.name ELSE null END) AS collection_name,
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN col2.name IS NOT NULL THEN col2.name ELSE null END) AS collection_name_optional,

  // Cast Actors (distinct)
  collect(DISTINCT p.name) AS cast_actors,

  // Crew Members (distinct)
  collect(DISTINCT p.name) AS crew_members,

  // User Ratings (Movie)
  collect(DISTINCT u.username) AS users_rating_movie,

  // Video Titles (if exists)
  IF EXISTS(video_titles) THEN [video_titles] ELSE [] END AS video_titles,

  // User Ratings (Video)
  collect(DISTINCT CASE WHEN users_rating_video IS NOT NULL THEN u.username ELSE null END) AS users_rating_video
```

### Key Improvements:
1. **Distinct Handling**: Uses `CASE WHEN` to ensure distinct values across optional matches.
2. **Structured Output**: Separates core and optional fields clearly.
3. **Video Handling**: Explicitly checks for `video_titles` existence.
4. **Optional Match**: Properly handles cases where relationships might not exist.

### Alternative (Simpler) Approach:
If you prefer a more concise version without all optional fields, you could use:
```cypher
MATCH (m:Movie)-[r:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(l:Language),
      (m)-[r:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(c:Country),
      (m)-[r:IN_GENRE]->(g:Genre),
      (m)-[r:PRODUCED_BY]->(pc:ProductionCompany),
      (m)-[r:HAS_KEYWORD]->(k:Keyword),
      (m)-[r:IN_COLLECTION]->(col:Collection),
      (p:Person)-[r:CAST_FOR]->m,
      (u:User)-[r:RATED]->m
RETURN m.title AS movie_title,
       collect(distinct l.name) AS languages,
       c.name AS production_country,
       collect(distinct g.name) AS genres,
       pc.name AS production_company,
       collect(distinct k.name) AS keywords,
       col.name AS collection_name,
       collect(distinct p.name) AS cast_actors,
       collect(distinct u.username) AS users_rating_movie;
```

Choose based on whether you need optional video/crew details or a simpler core result.

cypher:
The provided Cypher query is excellent and addresses the prompt's requirements effectively. Here's a breakdown of why it's good and some minor suggestions for potential improvements, focusing on readability and maintainability:

**Strengths:**

* **Comprehensive Coverage:** It covers all the necessary relationships between movies, languages, countries, genres, production companies, keywords, collections, and people.
* **Handles Optional Relationships:** The `OPTIONAL MATCH` blocks gracefully handle cases where some relationships might not exist for a given movie.
* **Distinct Value Handling:** Using `collect(DISTINCT ...)` ensures that the output lists contain only unique values, avoiding redundancy.
* **Clear Structure:** The query is well-organized, with distinct sections for core movie data, cast/crew, and user ratings.
* **Readability:** The use of aliases (e.g., `l` for Language, `c` for Country) improves readability.
* **Correctness:** The query is likely to produce accurate results based on the provided schema.

**Minor Suggestions (Primarily for Readability/Maintainability):**

1. **More Descriptive Variable Names:** While the aliases are helpful, consider slightly more descriptive variable names where appropriate. For example, `l2` could be `optional_language`.

2. **Comments for Complex Logic:**  The `CASE WHEN` statements within the `collect` functions can be a bit dense. Adding comments to explain the logic behind each `CASE` branch would improve maintainability.

3. **Consider Using `WITH` for Intermediate Results:**  For more complex queries, using the `WITH` clause to create intermediate variables can improve readability and reduce the size of the query plan.  This is particularly useful when you're collecting distinct values.

4. **Simplify `IF EXISTS` Check:** The `IF EXISTS(video_titles) THEN [video_titles] ELSE [] END` construct is a bit verbose.  A more concise alternative is `video_titles IS NOT NULL THEN video_titles ELSE [] END`.

**Revised Query (Incorporating Some Suggestions):**

```cypher
MATCH (m:Movie)-[r1:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(l:Language),
      (m)-[r2:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(c:Country),
      (m)-[r3:IN_GENRE]->(g:Genre),
      (m)-[r4:PRODUCED_BY]->(pc:ProductionCompany),
      (m)-[r5:HAS_KEYWORD]->(k:Keyword),
      (m)-[r6:IN_COLLECTION]->(col:Collection),
      (p:Person)-[r7:CAST_FOR]->m AS cast_actors,
      (p)-[r8:CREW_FOR]->m AS crew_members,
      (u:User)-[r9:RATED]->m AS users_rating_movie,
      OPTIONAL MATCH (m)-[r10:ORIGINAL_LANGUAGE|SPOKEN_IN_LANGUAGE]->(l2:Language),
      (m)-[r11:PRODUCED_IN_COUNTRY]->(c2:Country),
      (m)-[r12:IN_GENRE]->(g2:Genre),
      (m)-[r13:PRODUCED_BY]->(pc2:ProductionCompany),
      (m)-[r14:HAS_KEYWORD]->(k2:Keyword),
      (m)-[r15:IN_COLLECTION]->(col2:Collection),
      (p:Person)-[r16:CREW_FOR]->(v:Video) AS video_crew,
      (u:User)-[r17:RATED]->(v:Video) AS users_rating_video,
      (v)-[:TITLE]->(v_title:Video) WITH v_title.title AS video_titles,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN l2.name IS NOT NULL THEN l2.name ELSE null END) AS languages_optional,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c2.name IS NOT NULL THEN c2.name ELSE null END) AS production_country_optional,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN g2.name IS NOT NULL THEN g2.name ELSE null END) AS genres_optional,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN pc2.name IS NOT NULL THEN pc2.name ELSE null END) AS production_company_optional,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN k2.name IS NOT NULL THEN k2.name ELSE null END) AS keywords_optional,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN col2.name IS NOT NULL THEN col2.name ELSE null END) AS collection_name_optional,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT p.name) AS cast_actors,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT p.name) AS crew_members,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT u.username) AS users_rating_movie,
      OPTIONAL IF EXISTS(video_titles) THEN [video_titles] ELSE [] END AS video_titles,
      OPTIONAL COLLECT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN users_rating_video IS NOT NULL THEN u.username ELSE null END) AS users_rating_video

RETURN m.title AS movie_title,
       languages,
       production_country,
       genres,
       production_company,
       keywords,
       collection_name,
       cast_actors,
       crew_members,
       users_rating_movie,
       video_titles,
       users_rating_video;
```

**In summary, the original query is already very good. These suggestions are primarily aimed at enhancing readability and maintainability, especially for more complex queries.**
